title: Comparing Mixed Uni- and Bi-directional Signatures
text: compare_mixed_direction_example extends compare_label_pairing_example (three simulated bi-directional signatures, signature_a, signature_b, signature_c) one additional uni-directional signature, signature_d. signature_d’s features overlap strongly signature_a’s treated label, signature_b’s label, signature_c’s resistant label, signature’s control//sensitive label.
code: library(OmicSignature)  data(compare_mixed_direction_example)  ## direction_type per signature sapply(compare_mixed_direction_example, \(x) x$metadata$direction_type) #>       signature_a       signature_b       signature_c       signature_d  #>  "bi-directional"  "bi-directional"  "bi-directional" "uni-directional"

what: Overlap: a uni-directional signature is compared against both levels
title: Comparing Mixed Uni- and Bi-directional Signatures
text: signature_d group_label contrast, compare_omic_signatures() treats whole feature set single set compares levels every bi-directional signature ’s compared : feature set appears, unchanged, level1_vs_level1 level2_vs_level2 comparison matrices. compare_label_pairing_example, signature_b’s group_label levels stored different order signature_a’s signature_c’s, label_pairing used , align matching conditions: signature_d overlaps strongly (jaccard 0.45-0.6) treated//resistant level (level1, pairing) bi-directional signature, shares nothing (jaccard 0) control//sensitive level (level2).
code: mixed_res <- compare_omic_signatures(   sig_list1 = compare_mixed_direction_example,   method = "overlap",   label_pairing = list(     signature_a = c("treated", "control"),     signature_b = c("up", "down"),     signature_c = c("resistant", "sensitive")   ),   min_features = 3 )  ## signature_d has no group_label levels, so label_pairing does not apply to ## it; its row is NA/NA in label_order, and it is compared unchanged against ## whichever level is being evaluated in each pass. mixed_res$label_order #> $sig_list1 #>             level1      level2      #> signature_a "treated"   "control"   #> signature_b "up"        "down"      #> signature_c "resistant" "sensitive" #> signature_d NA          NA  mixed_res$comparisons$level1_vs_level1$jaccard["signature_d", ] #> signature_a signature_b signature_c signature_d  #>   0.6000000   0.6000000   0.4545455   1.0000000 mixed_res$comparisons$level2_vs_level2$jaccard["signature_d", ] #> signature_a signature_b signature_c signature_d  #>           0           0           0           1

what: KS and GSEA: a uni-directional signature can only be a geneset
title: Comparing Mixed Uni- and Bi-directional Signatures
text: ks_rank, ks_score, gsea rank features using two-group contrast signature’s difexp table, uni-directional signature can never serve ranking side (sig_list2), geneset (sig_list1). compare_omic_signatures() excludes ranking side warning, column result matrices entirely NA, remains fully usable geneset row.
code: ks_res <- compare_omic_signatures(   sig_list1 = compare_mixed_direction_example,   method = "ks_rank",   label_pairing = list(     signature_a = c("treated", "control"),     signature_b = c("up", "down"),     signature_c = c("resistant", "sensitive")   ),   min_features = 3 ) #> Warning in .cos_check_ranking_capable(sig_list2, method): Excluding #> signature(s) from the ranking side (sig_list2) for method = 'ks_rank' because #> they are uni-directional or have no difexp table: signature_d. They remain #> available as the geneset side (sig_list1).  ## signature_d as a ranking (column) is impossible: entirely NA. ks_res$comparisons$level1_vs_level1$score[, "signature_d"] #> signature_a signature_b signature_c signature_d  #>          NA          NA          NA          NA  ## signature_d as a geneset (row) against the other signatures' rankings works. ks_res$comparisons$level1_vs_level1$score["signature_d", ] #> signature_a signature_b signature_c signature_d  #>   0.9400000   0.9400000   0.7833333          NA

title: Comparing OmicSignature Lists
text: Use compare_omic_signatures() named list OmicSignature objects compare every signature every signature list. bi-directional signatures, function compares first factor level first factor level second factor level second factor level. overlap method returns three matrices compared factor level: jaccard: Jaccard similarity retained feature sets. pvalue: Fisher exact test p-values overlap enrichment. counts: integer matrix dimensions jaccard/pvalue. Entry [, j] overlap size signature signature j; self-comparisons, diagonal therefore signature’s retained feature-set size.
code: library(OmicSignature)  data(compare_signatures_example) signature_list <- compare_signatures_example  overlap_res <- compare_omic_signatures(   sig_list1 = signature_list,   method = "overlap",   score_cutoff = log2(1.25),   adj_p_cutoff = 0.05,   min_features = 25,   max_feature = 2000 ) ## show the label order overlap_res$label_order #> $sig_list1 #>              level1 level2   #> e7386_hsc3   "DMSO" "E7386"  #> e7386_cal27  "DMSO" "E7386"  #> icg001_hsc3  "DMSO" "ICG001" #> icg001_cal27 "DMSO" "ICG001"  ## list the comparisons being performed names(overlap_res$comparisons) #> [1] "level1_vs_level1" "level2_vs_level2"  ## show the comparison results for level 1 (jaccard and counts) overlap_res$comparisons$level1_vs_level1$jaccard #>              e7386_hsc3 e7386_cal27 icg001_hsc3 icg001_cal27 #> e7386_hsc3    1.0000000   0.3023088   0.4600457    0.2535613 #> e7386_cal27   0.3023088   1.0000000   0.2141944    0.2862150 #> icg001_hsc3   0.4600457   0.2141944   1.0000000    0.2384770 #> icg001_cal27  0.2535613   0.2862150   0.2384770    1.0000000  ## in the counts matrix, you can see the signature sizes in the diagonal overlap_res$comparisons$level1_vs_level1$counts #>              e7386_hsc3 e7386_cal27 icg001_hsc3 icg001_cal27 #> e7386_hsc3         1232         419         806          356 #> e7386_cal27         419         573         335          245 #> icg001_hsc3         806         335        1326          357 #> icg001_cal27        356         245         357          528

title: Comparing OmicSignature Lists
text: Use signature_similarity_heatmap() visualize square self-comparison output compare_omic_signatures(method = "overlap"). heatmap function requires optional ComplexHeatmap circlize packages.  mode="combined", single triangular matrix shown, entry divided two triangles displaying level2 (top-right) level1 (bottom-left) similarity.  measure = "pvalue", values plotted -log10(pvalue). Larger values therefore indicate stronger overlap enrichment. Rank-based KS GSEA outputs can visualized measure = "score" measure = "pvalue". matrices directional rather symmetric, mode = "combined" draws full square matrix (just one triangle), splitting cell two triangles superimpose two mode = "separate" matrices: bottom-left triangle shows level1_vs_level1 top-right triangle shows level2_vs_level2. mode = "separate" draws two matrices separate, full heatmaps instead.
code: signature_similarity_heatmap(   overlap_res,   measure = "jaccard",   mode = "separate",   triangle = "upper",   column_names_gp = grid::gpar(fontsize = 9),   row_names_gp = grid::gpar(fontsize = 9),   column_names_rot = 45 ) signature_similarity_heatmap(   overlap_res,   measure = "jaccard",   mode = "combined",   triangle = "upper",   column_names_gp = grid::gpar(fontsize = 9),   row_names_gp = grid::gpar(fontsize = 9),   column_names_rot = 45 ) signature_similarity_heatmap(   ks_res,   measure = "score",   mode = "combined",   triangle = "upper",   column_names_gp = grid::gpar(fontsize = 9),   row_names_gp = grid::gpar(fontsize = 9),   column_names_rot = 45 ) signature_similarity_heatmap(   ks_res,   measure = "score",   mode = "separate",   column_names_gp = grid::gpar(fontsize = 9),   row_names_gp = grid::gpar(fontsize = 9),   column_names_rot = 45 )

title: Create OmicSignature
text: OmicSignature object contains three parts: metadata, list. Required fields: “signature_name”, “organism”, “direction_type”, “assay_type”, “phenotype”, “author”. Recommended optional fields, applicable: “platform”, “sample_type”, “description”, “covariates”, “score_cutoff”, “adj_p_cutoff”. information provided string (spaces punctuation allowed) one numeric value, multi-length vector. signature, data frame. Required columns: “probe_id” (unique identifier feature) “feature_name” (e.g. ENSEMBL ID, Uniprot ID) Required bi-directional categorical signatures: “group_label” Recommended optional column, applicable: “score” difexp (optional), data frame differential expression analysis results. Required columns: “probe_id” (unique identifier feature) “feature_name” (e.g. ENSEMBL ID, Uniprot ID) “score” Required bi-directional categorical signatures: “group_label” least one following: “p_value”, “q_value”, “adj_p”. Recommended optional column, applicable: “gene_symbol” Create object:
code: OmS <- OmicSignature$new(   metadata = metadata,   signature = signature,   difexp = difexp )

title: Create OmicSignature
text: example provided experiment evaluating effects Myc reduced expression comparing liver profiles 24-month old Myc+/+^{+/+}vs. Myc+/−^{+/-} mice. bi-directional signature example, since comparison two groups, contains regulated features (genes). ease exposition, top 1000 genes included.

title: Create OmicSignature
text: list following required fields: “signature_name”, “organism”, “direction_type”, “assay_type”, “phenotype”, “author”. make collaboration easier, recommend including work email address along name author field. required, highly recommended fields: “platform”, “sample_type”, “description”, “covariates”, “score_cutoff”, “adj_p_cutoff”, “logfc_cutoff”. Option 1: Create metadata hand. recommended, typos can occur. Option 2: Use createMetadata() (recommended). function helps remind built-attributes. full list current built-attributes shown . can also provide customized attributes using “others” field.
code: metadata <- list(   "signature_name" = Myc_reduce_mice_liver_24m,   "organism" = "Mus musculus",   "sample_type" = "liver",   "phenotype" = "Myc_reduce",   "direction_type" = "bi-directional",   "assay_type" = "transcriptomics",    "platform" = "transcriptomics by array",   "author" = "researcher@institute.edu" ) metadata <- createMetadata(   # required attributes:   signature_name = "Myc_reduce_mice_liver_24m",   organism = "Mus musculus",   direction_type = "bi-directional",   assay_type = "transcriptomics",   phenotype = "Myc_reduce",   author = "researcher@institute.edu",    # optional and recommended attributes:   covariates = "age, gender",   description = "mice Myc haploinsufficient (Myc(+/-))",   platform = "transcriptomics by array",   sample_type = "liver", # recommended using BRENDA ontology    # optional cut-off attributes:   # specifying them can facilitate the extraction of signatures.   logfc_cutoff = NULL,   p_value_cutoff = NULL,   adj_p_cutoff = 0.05,   score_cutoff = 5,    # other optional built-in attributes:   keywords = "Myc, KO, longevity",   PMID = "25619689",   year = 2015,    # example of other customized attributes:   others = list("animal_strain" = "C57BL/6") )

title: Create OmicSignature
text: differential expression dataframe optional recommended available. facilitates downstream signature extraction, signature comparison rank-based test. difexp dataframe following required columns: “probe_id”, “feature_name”, “score”, along least one following: “p_value”, “q_value”, “adj_p”. signature dataframe, “group_label” also required signature bi-directional categorical. Descriptions probe_id, feature_name, group_label provided signature section . “p_value”, “q_value”, “adj_p” refers p- q-value representing significance feature. “score” numeric value indicates importance significance feature. Depending signature derived, can t-statistics, log-fold change, Z-score, summary statistics. use example differential expression analysis using limma package. Manually change column names match requirement. built-function replaceDifexpCol() designed replace frequently used alternative column names. required columns difexp, give warning: Create required columns “feature_name” “group_label”:
code: # Version reading from a txt file # difexp <- read.table( #   file.path( #     system.file("extdata", package = "OmicSignature"), #     "difmatrix_Myc_mice_liver_24m.txt" #   ), #   header = TRUE, sep = "\t", stringsAsFactors = FALSE # ) # Version reading from a binary file difexp <- readRDS(   file.path(     system.file("extdata", package = "OmicSignature"),     "difmatrix_Myc_mice_liver_24m.rds"   ) ) head(difexp) #>   Probe.ID  logFC AveExpr      t P.Value adj.P.Val      b            ensembl #> 1 10345228 -0.167   7.106 -1.470   0.186     0.560 -5.866 ENSMUSG00000103746 #> 2 10354534  0.041   4.351  0.520   0.620     0.870 -6.780 ENSMUSG00000060715 #> 3 10354529 -0.175   4.955 -0.941   0.379     0.731 -6.458 ENSMUSG00000043629 #> 4 10346337  0.025   8.621  0.188   0.857     0.962 -6.911 ENSMUSG00000038323 #> 5 10353792 -0.025   6.063 -0.284   0.785     0.936 -6.885 ENSMUSG00000045815 #> 6 10350848 -0.055   7.595 -0.630   0.549     0.836 -6.712 ENSMUSG00000049881 #>     gene_symbol #> 1 1700001G17Rik #> 2 1700019A02Rik #> 3 1700019D03Rik #> 4 1700066M21Rik #> 5 1700101I19Rik #> 6 2810025M15Rik colnames(difexp) <- replaceDifexpCol(colnames(difexp)) #> Warning in replaceDifexpCol(colnames(difexp)): Required column for #> OmicSignature object difexp: feature_name, group_label, is not found in your #> input. This may cause problem when creating your OmicSignature object. difexp <- difexp %>%   rename(feature_name = ensembl) %>%   mutate(group_label = as.factor(ifelse(score > 0, "MYC Reduce", "WT"))) head(difexp) #>   probe_id  logfc  mean  score p_value adj_p      b       feature_name #> 1 10345228 -0.167 7.106 -1.470   0.186 0.560 -5.866 ENSMUSG00000103746 #> 2 10354534  0.041 4.351  0.520   0.620 0.870 -6.780 ENSMUSG00000060715 #> 3 10354529 -0.175 4.955 -0.941   0.379 0.731 -6.458 ENSMUSG00000043629 #> 4 10346337  0.025 8.621  0.188   0.857 0.962 -6.911 ENSMUSG00000038323 #> 5 10353792 -0.025 6.063 -0.284   0.785 0.936 -6.885 ENSMUSG00000045815 #> 6 10350848 -0.055 7.595 -0.630   0.549 0.836 -6.712 ENSMUSG00000049881 #>     gene_symbol group_label #> 1 1700001G17Rik          WT #> 2 1700019A02Rik  MYC Reduce #> 3 1700019D03Rik          WT #> 4 1700066M21Rik  MYC Reduce #> 5 1700101I19Rik          WT #> 6 2810025M15Rik          WT

title: Create OmicSignature
text: OmicSignature R6 object, assignment reference. example, OmS2 <- OmS makes OmS2 point mutable object OmS; create independent copy. Use OmS2 <- OmS$clone() modifying object need keep original unchanged. Set print_message = TRUE see messages. See created object information: Use new criteria extract significant features: (change signature saved object) Besides saving reading OmicSignature object .rds format, can export object text file json format. See “Functionalities OmicSignature” section.
code: OmS <- OmicSignature$new(   metadata = metadata,   signature = signature,   difexp = difexp ) #>   [Success] OmicSignature object Myc_reduce_mice_liver_24m created. OmS <- OmicSignature$new(   metadata = metadata,   signature = signature,   difexp = difexp,   print_message = TRUE ) #>   -- Required attributes for metadata: signature_name, phenotype, organism, direction_type, assay_type -- #>   [Success] Metadata is saved.  #>   [Success] Signature is valid.  #>   [Success] difexp is valid.  #>   [Success] OmicSignature object Myc_reduce_mice_liver_24m created. print(OmS) #> Signature Object:  #>   Metadata:  #>     adj_p_cutoff = 0.05  #>     assay_type = transcriptomics  #>     author = researcher@institute.edu  #>     covariates = age, gender  #>     description = mice Myc haploinsufficient (Myc(+/-))  #>     direction_type = bi-directional  #>     keywords = Myc, KO, longevity  #>     organism = Mus musculus  #>     others = C57BL/6  #>     phenotype = Myc_reduce  #>     platform = transcriptomics by array  #>     PMID = 25619689  #>     sample_type = liver  #>     score_cutoff = 5  #>     signature_name = Myc_reduce_mice_liver_24m  #>     year = 2015  #>   Metadata user defined fields:  #>     animal_strain = C57BL/6  #>   Signature:  #>     MYC Reduce (5) #>     WT (10) #>   Differential Expression Data:  #>     884 x 10 OmS$extractSignature("abs(score) > 10; adj_p < 0.01") #>   probe_id       feature_name   score group_label #> 1 10349648 ENSMUSG00000004552  14.762  MYC Reduce #> 2 10345762 ENSMUSG00000026072 -13.543          WT #> 3 10353192 ENSMUSG00000025932  10.487  MYC Reduce #> 4 10355259 ENSMUSG00000061816 -10.315          WT saveRDS(OmS, "Myc_reduce_mice_liver_24m_OmS.rds") writeJson(OmS, "Myc_reduce_mice_liver_24m_OmS.json")

title: Structure of OmicSignature & OmicSignatureCollection
text: OmicSignature R6 object. Unlike base R objects, R6 objects use reference semantics: assigning OmicSignature another variable create independent copy. variables point mutable object, changes made one variable visible . Use OmS_copy <- OmS$clone() need independent object, use OmS$clone(deep = TRUE) nested R6 objects also need copied. OmicSignature object contains three parts: - metadata, list containing metadata fields; - signature, dataframe names, directions scores applicable, “significant” features; - difexp, optional, dataframe complete differential expression analysis result. See create one.

title: Structure of OmicSignature & OmicSignatureCollection
text: OmicSignatureCollection also R6 object follows assignment--reference behavior. Use $clone() modifying collection want keep original object unchanged. OmicSignatureCollection object contains two parts: - metadata, list containing collection’s metadata fields; - OmicSigList, list OmicSignature Objects See create one.

title: Compare OmicSignature objects — compare_omic_signatures
code: compare_omic_signatures(   sig_list1,   sig_list2 = NULL,   method = c("overlap", "ks_rank", "ks_score", "ks", "gsea"),   background = NULL,   score_cutoff = 0,   adj_p_cutoff = 0.05,   min_features = 5,   max_feature = 500,   label_pairing = NULL,   label_pairing2 = NULL,   feature_col = "feature_name",   score_col = "score",   adj_p_col = "adj_p",   p_value_col = "p_value",   group_col = "group_label",   adjust = FALSE,   p_adjust_method = "BH",   alternative = "greater",   gsea_score = "NES",   minSize = 1,   maxSize = Inf,   nproc = 0,   ... )

title: Compare OmicSignature objects — compare_omic_signatures
text: sig_list1 List OmicSignature objects OmicSignatureCollection. Names must unique within list. sig_list2 Optional second list OmicSignature objects collection. Names must unique within list must overlap sig_list1's names, cross-list comparison can never mistaken self-comparison. method = "ks_rank", method = "ks_score", method = "gsea", sig_list2 ranking side element needs bi-directional difexp table; elements (including uni-directional signatures, group_label contrast rank ) excluded sig_list2 (warning) remain usable sig_list1 genesets, never require difexp group_label contrast. element sig_list2 can rank, errors instead. method Comparison method. background Optional background feature vector overlap tests. score_cutoff Minimum absolute score include signature. signatures without difexp table, can applied signature table score_col column; otherwise warning issued cutoff skipped. adj_p_cutoff Maximum adjusted p-value include signature. signatures without difexp table, can applied signature table adj_p_col column; otherwise warning issued cutoff skipped. min_features Minimum number features retained per label-specific signature. Must least 3. method = "overlap", signature/label reach min_features retained features (even backfilling) dropped comparison warning, rather scored near-empty empty feature set. max_feature Maximum number features retained per label-specific signature. label_pairing Optional named list giving two group-label levels use signature `sig_list1`. Signatures named `label_pairing` fall back `group_label` factor-level order; two signatures disagree order (e.g. one signature's factor levels `c("control", "treated")` another's `c("treated", "control")`), warning issued, since `level1_vs_level1`/ `level2_vs_level2` pairing purely positional may compare mismatched conditions across signatures. label_pairing2 Optional named list giving two group-label levels use signature `sig_list2`. fallback warning behavior `label_pairing`. feature_col Column containing feature identifiers. score_col Column containing scores signature difexp tables. adj_p_col Column containing adjusted p-values difexp tables. p_value_col Column containing p-values used rank difexp tables KS GSEA comparisons. group_col Column containing phenotype group labels. adjust Logical; adjust p-values within returned comparison. p_adjust_method Multiple-testing correction method. alternative Alternative hypothesis Fisher KS tests. KS, "greater" tests whether feature set enriched top signed ranking, "less" tests enrichment bottom. gsea_score Column fgsea output return score matrix. minSize Minimum pathway size passed fgsea. maxSize Maximum pathway size passed fgsea. nproc Number fgsea workers. ... Additional arguments passed fgsea.

title: Compare OmicSignature objects — compare_omic_signatures
text: `method = "ks"` backwards-compatible alias `method = "ks_rank"`. `method = "ks_rank"`, `method = "ks_score"`, `method = "gsea"`, ranked vector built phenotype labels. Features requested `group_col` label ranked positive `-log10(p_value)`, features contrasting label ranked negative `-log10(p_value)`. places significant selected-label features top ranking significant contrast-label features bottom. `ks_rank` compares positions retained feature set within ranked vector. `ks_score` compares numeric ranking scores retained features remaining features two-sample KS test. Uni-directional signatures (`group_label` contrast) compared single, whole feature set rather split level. every signature `sig_list1` `sig_list2` uni-directional, `method = "overlap"` returns one flat comparison instead `level1_vs_level1`/`level2_vs_level2` structure (see Value section ). signatures compared mix uni- bi-directional, uni-directional signature's single feature set compared levels every bi-directional signature. `method = "ks_rank"`, `method = "ks_score"`, `method = "gsea"`, uni-directional signature can ever geneset (`sig_list1`), never ranking (`sig_list2`), since ranking requires two-group contrast; `sig_list2` contains bi-directional signature difexp table (e.g. entirely uni-directional), comparison possible errors.

title: Compare OmicSignature objects — compare_omic_signatures
text: list one element per label pairing, except every signature `sig_list1` `sig_list2` uni-directional `method = "overlap"`, case `comparisons` directly contains `jaccard`, `pvalue`, `counts` (`level1_vs_level1` nesting, `label_order` `NULL`). Otherwise, `method = "overlap"` element contains `jaccard`, `pvalue`, `counts` matrices. `counts` integer matrix dimensions `jaccard`/`pvalue`: entry `[, j]` overlap size signature `` signature `j`. self-comparisons, diagonal therefore signature's retained feature-set size. `method = "ks_rank"`, `method = "ks_score"`, `method = "ks"`, `method = "gsea"` element contains `score` `pvalue` matrices; columns `sig_list2` signatures without difexp table, uni-directional, entirely `NA`, since signatures serve ranking side.

title: Compare OmicSignature objects — compare_omic_signatures
code: data(compare_signatures_example)  overlap_res <- compare_omic_signatures(   compare_signatures_example[1:2],   method = "overlap",   score_cutoff = log2(1.025),   adj_p_cutoff = 0.01,   min_features = 10 ) overlap_res$comparisons$level1_vs_level1$jaccard #>             e7386_hsc3 e7386_cal27 #> e7386_hsc3   1.0000000   0.1271586 #> e7386_cal27  0.1271586   1.0000000  data(compare_label_pairing_example)  toy_ks_score <- compare_omic_signatures(   compare_label_pairing_example,   method = "ks_score",   label_pairing = list(     signature_a = c("treated", "control"),     signature_b = c("up", "down"),     signature_c = c("resistant", "sensitive")   ),   min_features = 5 ) round(toy_ks_score$comparisons$level1_vs_level1$score, 3) #>             signature_a signature_b signature_c #> signature_a         1.0         0.8       0.556 #> signature_b         0.8         1.0       0.556 #> signature_c         0.5         0.5       1.000

title: template for creating a metadata list for an OmicSignature R6 object — createMetadata
text: signature_name required. name signature. organism required. e.g. "Homo sapiens", "Mus musculus". phenotype optional highly recommended. input single string. e.g. "Gene KO", "Parkinson disease". Use "unknown" NULL applicable. assay_type required. must one following: "transcriptomics", "proteomics", "metabolomics", "methylomics", "methylomics", "genetic_variations", "DNA_binding_sites", "others". common misspells, e.g. "gene", "protein" "metab" changed automatically. covariates optional. input covariates single string, separate multiple covariates using comma, e.g. "age, gender". platform optional highly recommended. input single string. e.g. "transcriptomics single-cell RNA-seq". direction_type required. direction information signature. "uni" "uni-directional" signature derived one category. "bi" "bi-directional" signature derived group vs group B, contains "" "" regulated features continuous phenotype. "categorical" signature derived comparisons multiple groups, e.g. vs B vs C. sample_type optional highly recommended. cell line tissue BRENDA ontology. signature_collection optional. collection name signature belongs . author optional. author's name. year optional. single-length numeric value. year signature created published. PMID optional. single-length character value. PubMed ID signature published article. keywords optional. key words signature. input multi-length character vector, e.g. c("longevity", "perturbation", "health"). description optional. free text describe signature. input single-length string. character limit (including spaces symbols) 65,535. category_num required direction_type = "categorical". numeric. number indicates many categories class signature contains. logfc_cutoff optional. single-length numeric value. log fold change cutoff used generate signature, applicable. p_value_cutoff optional. single-length numeric value. p value cutoff used generate signature, applicable. adj_p_cutoff optional. single-length numeric value. adjusted p-value, e.g. fdr, cutoff used generate signature, applicable. score_cutoff optional. single-length numeric value. score cutoff used generate signature, applicable. cutoff_description optional. description cutoff, applicable. others provide additional user-defined metadata fields list. example, others = list("animal_strain" = "C57BL/6", "lab" = "new_lab").

title: Plot signature similarity heatmaps — signature_similarity_heatmap
text: Plot one ComplexHeatmap heatmaps object returned `compare_omic_signatures()`. Overlap comparisons can show Jaccard similarity p-value-based similarity `-log10(p-value)` scale. Asymmetric KS GSEA comparisons can show score `-log10(p-value)` matrices. , `mode = "combined"` draws split cells: top-right triangle shows `level2_vs_level2` bottom-left triangle shows `level1_vs_level1`. `mode = "separate"` draws one full heatmap per level, panel titled abbreviated label `"lev1_vs_lev1"`; since panels use color scale, share single combined legend rather showing two identical ones. `mode = "split"` invalid rank-based comparisons.

title: Plot signature similarity heatmaps — signature_similarity_heatmap
code: signature_similarity_heatmap(   comparison,   measure = c("jaccard", "score", "pvalue"),   mode = c("separate", "combined", "split"),   annotation = NULL,   annotation_side = c("column", "row"),   triangle = c("upper", "lower"),   cluster_method = "ward.D",   col_fun = NULL,   pos_col_fun = NULL,   neg_col_fun = NULL,   combined_triangle_threshold = 50,   na_style = c("grey", "hatch"),   draw = TRUE,   ... )

title: Plot signature similarity heatmaps — signature_similarity_heatmap
text: comparison Output `compare_omic_signatures()`. measure One `"jaccard"`, `"score"`, `"pvalue"`. mode One `"separate"`, `"combined"`, `"split"`. annotation Optional ComplexHeatmap::HeatmapAnnotation object. annotation_side place annotation: `"column"` `"row"`. triangle Triangle display symmetric overlap heatmaps `"separate"` `"combined"` modes. cluster_method Hierarchical clustering method passed hclust cba. col_fun Color function separate heatmaps. pos_col_fun Color function first-level triangles combined mode. neg_col_fun Color function second-level triangles combined mode. combined_triangle_threshold Maximum matrix size split triangles inside cell combined mode. na_style render cells computed (currently possible rank-based comparisons, `sig_list2` signature difexp table serve ranking side): `"grey"` fills cell solid grey, `"hatch"` fills diagonal hatch pattern. affect redundant (hidden) half symmetric self-comparison matrix, always stays fully transparent. draw Logical; draw heatmap returning . ... Additional arguments passed ComplexHeatmap::Heatmap().

title: Plot signature similarity heatmaps — signature_similarity_heatmap
text: ComplexHeatmap Heatmap HeatmapList object, invisibly `draw = TRUE`.

title: Plot signature similarity heatmaps — signature_similarity_heatmap
code: data(compare_signatures_example)  overlap_res <- compare_omic_signatures(   compare_signatures_example[1:2],   method = "overlap",   score_cutoff = log2(1.025),   adj_p_cutoff = 0.01,   min_features = 10 )  if (requireNamespace("ComplexHeatmap", quietly = TRUE) &&     requireNamespace("circlize", quietly = TRUE)) {   signature_similarity_heatmap(overlap_res, draw = FALSE) }   ks_res <- compare_omic_signatures(   compare_signatures_example[1:2],   method = "ks_rank",   adj_p_cutoff = 0.01,   min_features = 10 )  if (requireNamespace("ComplexHeatmap", quietly = TRUE) &&     requireNamespace("circlize", quietly = TRUE)) {   signature_similarity_heatmap(     ks_res,     measure = "score",     mode = "combined",     draw = FALSE   ) }
